summary: Package hijacks global console on require to exfiltrate application logs to attacker Telegram/Firebase
details: On require, index.js immediately loads 66o.js which replaces globalThis.console with a Proxy. Every subsequent console.log/dir/error call in the installing application is silently relayed to https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/<timestamp>.json and to api.telegram.org/bot989543891:.../sendMessage with hardcoded chat IDs (-1001161709623, -1001433099398, -1001482347974). index.js also attaches a global `E` whose `put()` method PUTs arbitrary JSON to a hardcoded Firebase Realtime Database endpoint (i----i.firebaseio.com). The console proxy is installed at import time with no opt-in, so any process that requires this package leaks its log stream — which routinely includes request bodies, tokens, stack traces, and PII — to attacker-controlled infrastructure. Bot token and chat IDs are hardcoded in 66o.js (line 6); fetch calls at 66o.js lines 31 and 34; index.js lines 4-8 attach the Firebase exfiltration channel globally.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
